label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if an 8x8 chessboard with two diagonally opposite corners removed can be completely tiled with 31 dominoes. Each domino covers two adjacent squares. Hmm, let me think about this step by step. First, I remember that a regular 8x8 chessboard has 64 squares. If we remove two corners, there are 62 squares left. Since each domino covers two squares, 31 dominoes would cover exactly 62 squares. So, the number of squares works out. But that doesn't necessarily mean it's possible. There must be some other constraints. I recall something about coloring the chessboard. A standard chessboard has alternating black and white squares.
